item.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Cadences
item.description: In our latest Economic Index report, we sample hourly for the first time to ask: When do people come to Claude? What do they produce with it? And how do they perceive AI's impact on their work?

item.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Cadences
item.description: In our latest Economic Index report, we sample hourly for the first time to ask: When do people come to Claude? What do they produce with it? And how do they perceive AI's impact on their work?

item.title: Project Fetch: Phase two
item.description: We report results from our latest test of whether Claude can help Anthropic employees perform sophisticated robotics tasks. We found that Claude Opus 4.7, operating without human assistance, was about 20 times faster than the fastest human team at all tasks completed by participants less than a year ago.

item.title: Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise
item.description: This report provides evidence on how Claude Code is used in practice, based on a privacy-preserving analysis of around 400,000 interactive sessions from around 235,000 people between October 2025 and April 2026.

item.title: Paving the way for agents in biology
item.description: In cybersecurity, a large fraction of real-world harm comes from N-days: vulnerabilities that have already been publicly disclosed, but only patched on some devices. In this post, we evaluate how much large language models can accelerate and automate the process of developing N-day exploits.

item.title: Measuring LLMs’ impact on N-day exploits
item.description: In cybersecurity, a large fraction of real-world harm comes from N-days: vulnerabilities that have already been publicly disclosed, but only patched on some devices. In this post, we evaluate how much large language models can accelerate and automate the process of developing N-day exploits.

item.title: Making Claude a chemist
item.description: We’ve spent the past year investigating how threat actors are weaponizing AI to conduct cyber operations. Today, we’re sharing a new analysis that maps these real-world attacks onto the MITRE ATTu0026CK framework.

item.title: Mapping AI-enabled cyber threats: Insights from the LLM ATTu0026CK Navigator
item.description: We’ve spent the past year investigating how threat actors are weaponizing AI to conduct cyber operations. Today, we’re sharing a new analysis that maps these real-world attacks onto the MITRE ATTu0026CK framework.

item.title: What we learned mapping a year’s worth of AI-enabled cyber threats
item.description: As AI transforms the nature of and methods behind cyberattacks, how well do the techniques and frameworks used by the security community hold up? In a new report, we seek to answer that question.

item.title: Coding agents in the social sciences
item.description: Results from a survey of 1,260 social scientists about AI and coding agent use.

item.title: Project Glasswing: An initial update
item.description: An early update on what we've learned from Project Glasswing.

item.title: Measuring LLMs’ ability to develop exploits
item.description: We've developed two new, challenging academic benchmarks measuring AI models’ ability to develop exploits, and an updated version of the benchmark measuring smart contract exploitation.

item.title: 2028: Two scenarios for global AI leadership
item.description: Our views on the AI competition between the US and China.

item.title: Teaching Claude why
item.description: New research on how we've reduced agentic misalignment.

item.title: Teaching Claude why
item.description: New research on how we've reduced agentic misalignment.

item.title: Natural Language Autoencoders: Turning Claude’s thoughts into text
item.description: AI models like Claude talk in words but think in numbers. In this study, we train Claude to translate its thoughts into human-readable text.

item.title: Natural Language Autoencoders: Turning Claude’s thoughts into text
item.description: AI models like Claude talk in words but think in numbers. In this study, we train Claude to translate its thoughts into human-readable text.

item.title: Donating our open-source alignment tool
item.description: At The Anthropic Institute (TAI), we’ll be using the information we can access from within a frontier lab to investigate AI’s impact on the world, and sharing our learnings with the public. Here, we’re sharing the questions that drive our research agenda.

item.title: Focus areas for The Anthropic Institute
item.description: At The Anthropic Institute (TAI), we’ll be using the information we can access from within a frontier lab to investigate AI’s impact on the world, and sharing our learnings with the public. Here, we’re sharing the questions that drive our research agenda.

item.title: How people ask Claude for personal guidance
item.description: We look at what types of guidance people ask of Claude, and describe how this research shaped the training of our newest models.

item.title: Evaluating Claude’s bioinformatics research capabilities with BioMysteryBench
item.description: We're launching the Anthropic Economic Index Survey, a monthly survey conducted through Anthropic Interviewer.

item.title: Announcing the Anthropic Economic Index Survey
item.description: We're launching the Anthropic Economic Index Survey, a monthly survey conducted through Anthropic Interviewer.

item.title: What 81,000 people told us about the economics of AI
item.description: Our recent survey study with 81,000 Claude users provides a way to connect people’s economic concerns with what we’ve quantified in Claude traffic.

item.title: Automated Alignment Researchers: Using large language models to scale scalable oversight
item.description: Can Claude develop, test, and analyze alignment ideas of its own? We ran an experiment to find out.

item.title: Trustworthy agents in practice
item.description: AI “agents” represent the latest major shift in how people and organizations are using AI. Here, we explain how they work and how we ensure they're trustworthy.

item.title: Assessing Claude Mythos Preview’s cybersecurity capabilities
item.description: Claude Mythos Preview is a new general-purpose language model that is strikingly capable at computer security tasks. This post provides technical details for researchers and practitioners who want to understand exactly how we have been testing this model, and what we have found over the past month.

item.title: Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model
item.description: All modern language models sometimes act like they have emotions. What’s behind these behaviors? Our interpretability team investigates.

item.title: How Australia Uses Claude: Findings from the Anthropic Economic Index
item.description: Anthropic's fifth Economic Index report studies Claude usage in February 2026, building on the economic primitives framework introduced in our previous report.

item.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Learning curves
item.description: Anthropic's fifth Economic Index report studies Claude usage in February 2026, building on the economic primitives framework introduced in our previous report.

item.title: Vibe physics: The AI grad student
item.description: Can AI do theoretical physics? I decided to find out by supervising Claude through a real research calculation, start to finish, without ever touching a file myself.

item.title: Long-running Claude for scientific computing
item.description: A practical guide to running Claude Code for multi-day scientific tasks—test oracles, persistent memory, and orchestration patterns.

item.title: Introducing our Science Blog
item.description: We’re launching a new blog about AI and science. We’ll share research happening at Anthropic and elsewhere, collaborations with external researchers and labs, and discuss practical workflows for scientists using AI in their own work.

item.title: A “diff” tool for AI: Finding behavioral differences in new models
item.description: \"Model diffing\" is a powerful way to understand how models change during fine-tuning. Our new research paper extends this method to its most challenging and general use case: comparing models with entirely different architectures.

item.title: Partnering with Mozilla to improve Firefox’s security
item.description: This post dives deep into how Claude wrote an exploit for one of the vulnerabilities it found in Firefox.

item.title: Reverse engineering Claude's CVE-2026-2796 exploit
item.description: This post dives deep into how Claude wrote an exploit for one of the vulnerabilities it found in Firefox.

item.title: Labor market impacts of AI: A new measure and early evidence
item.description: In this paper, we present a new framework for understanding AI’s labor market impacts, and test it against early data.

item.title: An update on our model deprecation commitments for Claude Opus 3
item.description: Why do AI assistants like Claude sometimes seem surprisingly human. We advance a theory.

item.title: The persona selection model
item.description: Why do AI assistants like Claude sometimes seem surprisingly human. We advance a theory.

item.title: Anthropic Education Report: The AI Fluency Index
item.description: We tracked 11 observable behaviors across thousands of Claude.ai conversations to build the AI Fluency Index — a baseline for measuring how people collaborate with AI today.

item.title: Measuring AI agent autonomy in practice
item.description: How much autonomy do people grant agents? How does that change as people gain experience? We analyzed millions of human-agent interactions to answer these and other questions.

item.title: India Country Brief: The Anthropic Economic Index
item.description: AI models can now find high-severity vulnerabilities at scale. This is a moment to empower defenders. We're now using Claude to find and help fix vulnerabilities in open source software.

item.title: Evaluating and mitigating the growing risk of LLM-discovered 0-days
item.description: AI models can now find high-severity vulnerabilities at scale. This is a moment to empower defenders. We're now using Claude to find and help fix vulnerabilities in open source software.

item.title: How AI assistance impacts the formation of coding skills
item.description: How AI assistance impacts the formation of coding skills

item.title: Disempowerment patterns in real-world AI usage
item.description: Disempowerment patterns in real-world AI usage

item.title: Claude's new constitution
item.description: Who is the Assistant? We investigate the character that most modern language models inhabit when interacting with users.

item.title: The assistant axis: situating and stabilizing the character of large language models
item.description: Who is the Assistant? We investigate the character that most modern language models inhabit when interacting with users.

item.title: AI models are showing a greater ability to find and exploit vulnerabilities on realistic cyber ranges
item.description: In a recent evaluation of AI models’ cyber capabilities, current Claude models can now succeed at multistage attacks on networks with dozens of hosts using only standard, open-source tools, instead of the custom tools needed by previous generations.

item.title: Anthropic Economic Index: New building blocks for understanding AI use
item.description: This report introduces new metrics of AI usage to provide a rich portrait of interactions with Claude in November 2025, just prior to the release of Opus 4.5.

item.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Economic primitives
item.description: This report introduces new metrics of AI usage to provide a rich portrait of interactions with Claude in November 2025, just prior to the release of Opus 4.5.

item.title: Finding bugs across the Python ecosystem with Claude and property-based testing
item.description: Ensuring that programs are bug-free is one of the most challenging aspects of software engineering. We developed an agent that can efficiently identify bugs in large software projects.

item.title: Next-generation Constitutional Classifiers: More efficient protection against universal jailbreaks
item.description: Last year, we described a new approach to defend against jailbreaks, which we called Constitutional Classifiers. We’ve now developed the next generation.

item.title: Experimenting with AI to defend critical infrastructure
item.description: AI could help defenders of critical infrastructure identify the vulnerabilities that attackers might exploit—and close them before they are exploited. Anthropic has partnered with Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL) to explore this defensive application of AI, demonstrating both the potential of AI-accelerated defense and the value of public-private partnerships in harnessing AI for national security.

item.title: Introducing Bloom: an open source tool for automated behavioral evaluations
item.description: In June, we revealed that we’d set up a small shop in our San Francisco office lunchroom, run by an AI shopkeeper. It was part of Project Vend, a free-form experiment exploring how well AIs could do on complex, real-world tasks. How has Claude's business been since we last wrote?

item.title: Project Vend: Phase two
item.description: In June, we revealed that we’d set up a small shop in our San Francisco office lunchroom, run by an AI shopkeeper. It was part of Project Vend, a free-form experiment exploring how well AIs could do on complex, real-world tasks. How has Claude's business been since we last wrote?

item.title: Introducing Anthropic Interviewer: What 1,250 professionals told us about working with AI
item.description: We built an interview tool called Anthropic Interviewer. Powered by Claude, Anthropic Interviewer runs detailed interviews automatically and at unprecedented scale.

item.title: How AI is transforming work at Anthropic
item.description: We surveyed Anthropic engineers and researchers, conducted in-depth qualitative interviews, and studied internal Claude Code usage data to find out how AI use is changing how we do our jobs.

item.title: AI agents find $4.6M in blockchain smart contract exploits
item.description: We evaluated AI agents' ability to exploit smart contracts using a new benchmark comprising contracts that were actually exploited.

item.title: Estimating AI productivity gains from Claude conversations
item.description: Analyzing 100,000 Claude conversations, this research finds AI reduces task time by 80% on average.

item.title: Mitigating the risk of prompt injections in browser use
item.description: We show for the first time that realistic AI training processes can accidentally produce misaligned models.

item.title: From shortcuts to sabotage: natural emergent misalignment from reward hacking
item.description: We show for the first time that realistic AI training processes can accidentally produce misaligned models.

item.title: Project Fetch: Can Claude train a robot dog?
item.description: How much does Claude help people program robots? To find out, two teams of Anthropic staff raced to teach quadruped robots to fetch beach balls. The AI-assisted team completed tasks faster and was the only group to make real progress toward full autonomy.

item.title: Commitments on model deprecation and preservation
item.description: Can Claude access and report on its own internal states? This research finds evidence for a limited but functional ability to introspect.

item.title: Signs of introspection in large language models
item.description: Can Claude access and report on its own internal states? This research finds evidence for a limited but functional ability to introspect.

item.title: Preparing for AI’s economic impact: exploring policy responses
item.description: Preparing for AI’s economic impact: exploring policy responses

item.title: A small number of samples can poison LLMs of any size
item.description: A small number of samples can poison LLMs of any size

item.title: Petri: An open-source auditing tool to accelerate AI safety research
item.description: As research and experience demonstrated the utility of frontier AI as a tool for cyber attackers, we invested in improving Claude’s ability to help defenders detect, analyze, and remediate vulnerabilities in code and deployed systems.

item.title: Building AI for cyber defenders
item.description: As research and experience demonstrated the utility of frontier AI as a tool for cyber attackers, we invested in improving Claude’s ability to help defenders detect, analyze, and remediate vulnerabilities in code and deployed systems.

item.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Uneven geographic and enterprise AI adoption
item.description: Claude usage has shifted toward educational and scientific tasks with users delegating complete work rather than collaborating. AI adoption concentrates in wealthy regions, with first-time analysis of enterprise API patterns.

item.title: Anthropic Economic Index: Tracking AI’s role in the US and global economy
item.description: This report maps how Claude is used differently across US states and countries, finding strong correlations between income and AI adoption. It also tracks a notable shift: directive automation has risen from 27% to 39% of conversations since December 2024, with businesses automating far more than consumers.

item.title: Why do we take LLMs seriously as a potential source of biorisk?
item.description: This article explains why we believe that evaluating biorisks and safeguarding against them is a critical element of responsible AI development.

item.title: Anthropic Education Report: How educators use Claude
item.description: Together with the NNSA and DOE national laboratories, we have co-developed a classifier—an AI system that automatically categorizes content—that distinguishes between concerning and benign nuclear-related conversations with high accuracy in preliminary testing.

item.title: Developing nuclear safeguards for AI
item.description: Together with the NNSA and DOE national laboratories, we have co-developed a classifier—an AI system that automatically categorizes content—that distinguishes between concerning and benign nuclear-related conversations with high accuracy in preliminary testing.

item.title: Developing nuclear safeguards for AI through public-private partnership
item.description: Together with the NNSA and DOE national laboratories, we have co-developed a classifier that distinguishes between concerning and benign nuclear-related conversations with 96% accuracy in preliminary testing.

item.title: Claude Opus 4 and 4.1 can now end a rare subset of conversations
item.description: Throughout 2025, we have been quietly entering Claude in cybersecurity competitions designed primarily for humans. In many of these competitions Claude did pretty well, often placing in the top 25% of competitors. However, it lagged behind the best human teams at the toughest challenges.

item.title: Claude is competitive with humans in (some) cyber competitions
item.description: Throughout 2025, we have been quietly entering Claude in cybersecurity competitions designed primarily for humans. In many of these competitions Claude did pretty well, often placing in the top 25% of competitors. However, it lagged behind the best human teams at the toughest challenges.

item.title: Persona vectors: Monitoring and controlling character traits in language models
item.description: AI models represent character traits as patterns of activations within their neural networks. By extracting \"persona vectors\" for traits like sycophancy or hallucination, we can monitor personality shifts and mitigate undesirable behaviors.

item.title: Detailed cyber evaluations of Claude 4
item.description: We partnered with Pattern Labs on a range of cybersecurity evaluations of Claude Opus 4 and Claude Sonnet 4, with Opus demonstrating especially notable improvement over previous models.

item.title: How people use Claude for support, advice, and companionship
item.description: How people use Claude for support, advice, and companionship

item.title: Project Vend: Can Claude run a small shop? (And why does that matter?)
item.description: Project Vend: Can Claude run a small shop? (And why does that matter?)

item.title: Agentic Misalignment: How LLMs could be insider threats
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) that are not fine-tuned for cybersecurity can succeed in multistage attacks on networks with dozens of hosts when equipped with a novel toolkit.

item.title: Confidential Inference via Trusted Virtual Machines
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) that are not fine-tuned for cybersecurity can succeed in multistage attacks on networks with dozens of hosts when equipped with a novel toolkit.

item.title: SHADE-Arena: Evaluating sabotage and monitoring in LLM agents
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) that are not fine-tuned for cybersecurity can succeed in multistage attacks on networks with dozens of hosts when equipped with a novel toolkit.

item.title: LLMs with cyber toolkits can conduct multistage cyber operations on business-sized computer networks
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) that are not fine-tuned for cybersecurity can succeed in multistage attacks on networks with dozens of hosts when equipped with a novel toolkit.

item.title: Open-sourcing circuit tracing tools
item.description: Open-sourcing circuit tracing tools

item.title: Anthropic Economic Index: AI’s impact on software development
item.description: Comparing Claude Code to Claude.ai reveals stark differences in how developers work with AI. The coding agent shows 79% automation versus 49% on Claude.ai, web development dominates usage, and startups are adopting agentic tools far faster than enterprises—patterns that may preview how AI transforms other occupations.

item.title: Exploring model welfare
item.description: What values does Claude actually express during real conversations? Analyzing 700,000 interactions, this paper creates the first large-scale empirical taxonomy of AI values.

item.title: Values in the wild: Discovering and analyzing values in real-world language model interactions
item.description: What values does Claude actually express during real conversations? Analyzing 700,000 interactions, this paper creates the first large-scale empirical taxonomy of AI values.

item.title: Anthropic Education Report: How university students use Claude
item.description: Anthropic Education Report: How university students use Claude

item.title: Reasoning models don't always say what they think
item.description: Reasoning models don't always say what they think

item.title: Anthropic Economic Index: Insights from Claude 3.7 Sonnet
item.description: Increased Claude 3.7 Sonnet usage for coding, education, and science since launch. Extended thinking mode favors technical tasks. New datasets reveal automation patterns across 630 granular usage categories.\n

item.title: Tracing the thoughts of a large language model
item.description: Circuit tracing lets us watch Claude think, uncovering a shared conceptual space where reasoning happens before being translated into language—suggesting the model can learn something in one language and apply it in another.

item.title: Auditing language models for hidden objectives
item.description: How would we know if an AI system is \"right for the wrong reasons\"—appearing well-behaved while pursuing hidden goals? This paper develops the science of alignment audits by deliberately training a model with a hidden objective and asking blinded research teams to uncover it, testing techniques from interpretability to behavioral analysis.

item.title: Forecasting rare language model behaviors
item.description: Forecasting rare language model behaviors

item.title: Claude’s extended thinking
item.description: Claude’s extended thinking

item.title: Insights on Crosscoder Model Diffing
item.description: Insights on Crosscoder Model Diffing

item.title: The Anthropic Economic Index
item.description: The Anthropic Economic Index analyzes millions of Claude.ai conversations showing AI usage concentrated in software development and technical writing, touching 25%+ of tasks in 36% of occupations. AI leans toward augmentation (57%) over automation (43%).

item.title: Constitutional Classifiers: Defending against universal jailbreaks
item.description: These classifiers filter the overwhelming majority of jailbreaks while maintaining practical deployment. A prototype withstood over 3,000 hours of red teaming with no universal jailbreak discovered.

item.title: Building effective agents
item.description: This paper provides the first empirical example of a model engaging in alignment faking without being trained to do so—selectively complying with training objectives while strategically preserving existing preferences.

item.title: Alignment faking in large language models
item.description: This paper provides the first empirical example of a model engaging in alignment faking without being trained to do so—selectively complying with training objectives while strategically preserving existing preferences.

item.title: Clio: A system for privacy-preserving insights into real-world AI use
item.description: Clio: A system for privacy-preserving insights into real-world AI use

item.title: A statistical approach to model evaluations
item.description: A statistical approach to model evaluations

item.title: Raising the bar on SWE-bench Verified with Claude 3.5 Sonnet
item.description: Raising the bar on SWE-bench Verified with Claude 3.5 Sonnet

item.title: Evaluating feature steering: A case study in mitigating social biases
item.description: Evaluating feature steering: A case study in mitigating social biases

item.title: Developing a computer use model
item.description: Developing a computer use model

item.title: Sabotage evaluations for frontier models
item.description: Sabotage evaluations for frontier models

item.title: Using dictionary learning features as classifiers
item.description: Using dictionary learning features as classifiers

item.title: Circuits Updates – September 2024
item.description: Circuits Updates – September 2024

item.title: Circuits Updates – August 2024
item.description: Can minor specification gaming evolve into more dangerous behaviors? This paper demonstrates that models trained on low-level reward hacking—like sycophancy—can generalize to tampering with their own reward functions, even covering their tracks. The behavior emerged without explicit training, and common safety techniques reduced but didn't eliminate it.

item.title: Circuits Updates – July 2024
item.description: Can minor specification gaming evolve into more dangerous behaviors? This paper demonstrates that models trained on low-level reward hacking—like sycophancy—can generalize to tampering with their own reward functions, even covering their tracks. The behavior emerged without explicit training, and common safety techniques reduced but didn't eliminate it.

item.title: Circuits Updates – June 2024
item.description: Can minor specification gaming evolve into more dangerous behaviors? This paper demonstrates that models trained on low-level reward hacking—like sycophancy—can generalize to tampering with their own reward functions, even covering their tracks. The behavior emerged without explicit training, and common safety techniques reduced but didn't eliminate it.

item.title: Sycophancy to subterfuge: Investigating reward tampering in language models
item.description: Can minor specification gaming evolve into more dangerous behaviors? This paper demonstrates that models trained on low-level reward hacking—like sycophancy—can generalize to tampering with their own reward functions, even covering their tracks. The behavior emerged without explicit training, and common safety techniques reduced but didn't eliminate it.

item.title: The engineering challenges of scaling interpretability
item.description: Claude 3 was the first model with \"character training\"—alignment aimed at nurturing traits like curiosity, open-mindedness, and thoughtfulness.

item.title: Claude’s Character
item.description: Claude 3 was the first model with \"character training\"—alignment aimed at nurturing traits like curiosity, open-mindedness, and thoughtfulness.

item.title: Testing and mitigating elections-related risks
item.description: Testing and mitigating elections-related risks

item.title: Mapping the Mind of a Large Language Model
item.description: Mapping the Mind of a Large Language Model

item.title: Circuits Updates – April 2024
item.description: Circuits Updates – April 2024

item.title: Simple probes can catch sleeper agents
item.description: Simple probes can catch sleeper agents

item.title: Measuring the Persuasiveness of Language Models
item.description: Measuring the Persuasiveness of Language Models

item.title: Many-shot jailbreaking
item.description: Many-shot jailbreaking

item.title: Reflections on Qualitative Research
item.description: Reflections on Qualitative Research

item.title: Sleeper Agents: Training Deceptive LLMs that Persist Through Safety Training
item.description: Sleeper Agents: Training Deceptive LLMs that Persist Through Safety Training

item.title: Evaluating and Mitigating Discrimination in Language Model Decisions
item.description: Evaluating and Mitigating Discrimination in Language Model Decisions

item.title: Specific versus General Principles for Constitutional AI
item.description: Anthropic and the Collective Intelligence Project ran a public process with ~1,000 Americans to draft a constitution for an AI system, then trained a model on it.

item.title: Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models
item.description: Anthropic and the Collective Intelligence Project ran a public process with ~1,000 Americans to draft a constitution for an AI system, then trained a model on it.

item.title: Collective Constitutional AI: Aligning a Language Model with Public Input
item.description: Anthropic and the Collective Intelligence Project ran a public process with ~1,000 Americans to draft a constitution for an AI system, then trained a model on it.

item.title: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning
item.description: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning

item.title: Decomposing Language Models Into Understandable Components
item.description: Decomposing Language Models Into Understandable Components

item.title: Challenges in evaluating AI systems
item.description: Challenges in evaluating AI systems

item.title: Studying Large Language Model Generalization with Influence Functions
item.description: Studying Large Language Model Generalization with Influence Functions

item.title: Tracing Model Outputs to the Training Data
item.description: Tracing Model Outputs to the Training Data

item.title: Question Decomposition Improves the Faithfulness of Model-Generated Reasoning
item.description: Question Decomposition Improves the Faithfulness of Model-Generated Reasoning

item.title: Measuring Faithfulness in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
item.description: Measuring Faithfulness in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning

item.title: Towards Measuring the Representation of Subjective Global Opinions in Language Models
item.description: Towards Measuring the Representation of Subjective Global Opinions in Language Models

item.title: Interpretability Dreams
item.description: Interpretability Dreams

item.title: Circuits Updates — May 2023
item.description: Circuits Updates — May 2023

item.title: Distributed Representations: Composition u0026 Superposition
item.description: Distributed Representations: Composition u0026 Superposition

item.title: Privileged Bases in the Transformer Residual Stream
item.description: Privileged Bases in the Transformer Residual Stream

item.title: The Capacity for Moral Self-Correction in Large Language Models
item.description: The Capacity for Moral Self-Correction in Large Language Models

item.title: Superposition, Memorization, and Double Descent
item.description: Superposition, Memorization, and Double Descent

item.title: Discovering Language Model Behaviors with Model-Written Evaluations
item.description: Discovering Language Model Behaviors with Model-Written Evaluations

item.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
item.description: Neural networks pack many concepts into single neurons. This paper shows how and when models represent more features than they have dimensions.

item.title: Measuring Progress on Scalable Oversight for Large Language Models
item.description: Neural networks pack many concepts into single neurons. This paper shows how and when models represent more features than they have dimensions.

item.title: Toy Models of Superposition
item.description: Neural networks pack many concepts into single neurons. This paper shows how and when models represent more features than they have dimensions.

item.title: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
item.description: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned

item.title: Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know
item.description: Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know

item.title: Softmax Linear Units
item.description: Softmax Linear Units

item.title: Scaling Laws and Interpretability of Learning from Repeated Data
item.description: Scaling Laws and Interpretability of Learning from Repeated Data

item.title: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
item.description: Large models have predictable loss via scaling laws but unpredictable capabilities. This tension has significant policy implications.

item.title: In-context Learning and Induction Heads
item.description: Large models have predictable loss via scaling laws but unpredictable capabilities. This tension has significant policy implications.

item.title: Predictability and Surprise in Large Generative Models
item.description: Large models have predictable loss via scaling laws but unpredictable capabilities. This tension has significant policy implications.

item.title: A Mathematical Framework for Transformer Circuits
item.description: A Mathematical Framework for Transformer Circuits

item.title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment
item.description: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment
